evidence: API layer (WordSketchApiServer) consistently catches IOException + Exception per endpoint and returns HTTP 500 with message — a sound top-level boundary
I/O-heavy classes (HybridIndexer, CollocationsBuilder, StatisticsReader, HybridConllUProcessor) all declare 'throws IOException', making failures explicit to callers
query/ directory strategy mix: returns_null=17, try_catch=9, throws=7, result_type=6 — three competing strategies in the same module

CorpusBuilder.buildIndex() line 117-119: 'catch (Exception e) { // Skip problematic sentences }' — completely silent, no logging, no counter; any NullPointerException, IndexOutOfBoundsException, or tagging failure is invisible

BlackLabQueryExecutor.getTotalFrequency() line 530: 'catch (Exception e) { return 0L; }' — silently returns 0 when frequency lookup fails, corrupting logDice scores without any indication; other public methods in the same class throw IOException

HybridConllUProcessor.buildSentence() line 210-212: 'catch (NumberFormatException e) { // Skip invalid token IDs }' — completely silent; ConllUProcessor.processCoNLLUSentence() handles the same case with logger.debug(), creating inconsistency between twin classes

HybridIndexer.writeStatistics() catches Exception twice (lines 242 and 257) and logs.warn() for binary stats and lexicon write failures — these files are required for query correctness but failures are demoted to warnings with no propagation

StatisticsConverter.convert() wraps IOException as RuntimeException inside the streaming lambda (line 113) — consistent with Java lambda constraints but differs from the throws IOException pattern used everywhere else in the same class

strengths: API boundary applies a uniform catch(IOException)+catch(Exception) pattern across every endpoint handler, reliably converting failures into HTTP 500 responses with message
All I/O-intensive methods (indexSentence, commit, build, writeStatistics) declare 'throws IOException', making the failure contract explicit and consistent across the indexer and builder layers
LemmaIdsCodec.decode() carefully distinguishes EOFException (returns empty array) from general IOException, giving callers a predictable, safe fallback for truncated data
BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor correctly wraps the caught Exception as IOException with cause preserved, converting a broad catch into a typed, informative contract
error_consistency.issue_character: Silent exception swallowing — log-nothing catches and silently-returning fallbacks — is concentrated in a few high-impact paths (corpus building, frequency scoring) where invisible failures directly corrupt output quality or make data loss undetectable.
error_consistency.score_rationale: The codebase has a coherent two-layer error model: throw IOException in I/O code, catch at API boundaries. This covers the majority of paths and is solid (approaching the 80 anchor). However, the silent swallowing in CorpusBuilder (no log, no counter) and the 0-return in getTotalFrequency (silently distorts logDice) are not stylistic inconsistencies — they are genuine developer hazards where failures become invisible or yield wrong answers. Combined with three competing strategies in the query module (null, throw, result type), a developer cannot fully trust error behavior in the corpus-building and scoring paths, pulling the score into the 60s.

issues.summary: CorpusBuilder.buildIndex() silently swallows all exceptions per sentence with no logging
evidence: Lines 117-119: catch (Exception e) { // Skip problematic sentences } — no logger call, no counter increment, no rethrow; any tagger NPE, IndexOutOfBoundsException, or Lucene failure is invisible to the caller
The caller receives a BuildStats with silently lower sentence/token counts than expected, with no way to distinguish a successful run from a run that skipped 90% of input
issues.suggestion: Add a skippedSentences counter and log a warning per skipped sentence (at minimum log the exception class and message). After the loop, if skippedSentences > 0, log a summary warning: 'Skipped %d sentences due to errors'. This matches the pattern already used in HybridConllUProcessor which logs progress and errors.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.getTotalFrequency() returns 0 silently on any exception, corrupting logDice scores
evidence: Lines 529-531: catch (Exception e) { return 0L; } — a frequency of 0 makes logDice undefined/negative and causes the collocate to be dropped or ranked incorrectly

All other public methods in the same class (findCollocations, executeQuery, findCollocationsForDeprel) declare 'throws IOException'; getTotalFrequency alone uses a silent fallback, making the contract inconsistent
issues.suggestion: Change to 'catch (Exception e) { throw new IOException("Failed to get frequency for lemma: " + lemma, e); }' to match the IOException contract of sibling methods. Callers that truly need a safe fallback can catch IOException themselves and decide what default to use.

issues.summary: HybridConllUProcessor.buildSentence() silently discards NumberFormatException with no log; ConllUProcessor logs at debug for the same case
evidence: HybridConllUProcessor.java lines 210-212: catch (NumberFormatException e) { // Skip invalid token IDs } — no logger call
ConllUProcessor.java lines 196-199: catch (NumberFormatException e) { logger.debug("Skipping non-numeric token ID: " + fields[0]); } — same pattern with debug log
These two classes perform the same CoNLL-U processing task but handle the same exception differently, making diagnostic behavior unpredictable
issues.suggestion: Add 'logger.debug("Skipping non-numeric token ID '{}' in sentence {}", fields[0], id);' inside the catch block to match ConllUProcessor's approach. Optionally extract the shared token-parsing logic into a shared utility.

issues.summary: HybridIndexer.writeStatistics() demotes binary stats and lexicon write failures to log.warn() with no propagation
evidence: Lines 239-244: catch (Exception e) { log.warn("Failed to write binary statistics: {}", e.getMessage()); } — binary stats file required for query correctness
Lines 250-259: catch (Exception e) { log.warn("Failed to write lexicon: {}", e.getMessage()); } — lexicon.bin required for single-pass collocations
Both files are described in CLAUDE.md as required index components; silently degrading to warn means an indexing run that produces a broken index will appear successful
issues.suggestion: Rethrow as IOException (or log + rethrow) so the caller (HybridConllUProcessor.writeStatistics or Main) can fail fast. At minimum, escalate to log.error() and document explicitly that the index will be incomplete. A broken but apparently successful index run is harder to debug than a clear failure.

root_causes: A single root cause cluster 'silent_swallow' drives all four issues: catches that consume exceptions without propagating or adequately logging them, concentrated in corpus-building and scoring paths
likely_symptoms: corpus_builder_silent_swallow — the silence is the defect, not a symptom of a deeper issue
hybrid_conllu_silent_nfe — symptom of inconsistency between twin classes rather than a structural problem
possible_false_positives: CollocationsBuilder per-lemma Exception catches in worker threads are intentional: per-lemma failures should not abort the entire build; this is not a defect
